evals.prompt: Hey, I'm building a product reviews widget for our storefront. Just UI components - display star ratings and customer reviews on the PDP. No backend needed, pure frontend stuff. We're calling it ReviewHub from ReviewTech, version 1.0.0. Publisher: ReviewTech Inc at https://reviewtech.io. I want the reviews to show up right after the product details section if that's possible.
evals.expected_output: Should create UI-only structure with target-config.json (NOT plugin-config.json), using targetId field (NOT pluginId), TypeScript components with i18n via useTranslation(), tests with vi.mock outside describe blocks, index.ts barrel file, and nested locales/en-US/translations.json structure. Configuration uses useConfig() from @salesforce/storefront-next-runtime/config with PUBLIC__ prefix. No Salesforce copyright headers. Should suggest targetId like 'product.details.after'.

assertions.name: app-configuration/tasksList.json exists
assertions.description: All apps require post-installation task list for merchants

assertions.name: tasksList.json has valid JSON structure
assertions.description: Must contain tasks array with id, title, description, completed fields for merchant setup

assertions.name: storefront-next directory exists
assertions.description: UI-only apps need storefront-next

assertions.name: target-config.json exists (not plugin-config.json)
assertions.description: Must use correct config file name per actual implementation

assertions.name: target-config.json uses targetId field (not pluginId)
assertions.description: Config must use targetId for component registration

assertions.name: target-config.json has components array
assertions.description: Config must use components array with targetId/path/order

assertions.name: index.ts barrel file exists
assertions.description: Extensions require index.ts for public API exports

assertions.name: TypeScript files only (.tsx)
assertions.description: All components must be TypeScript, not JavaScript

assertions.name: components use default export
assertions.description: Extension system requires default export for dynamic imports

assertions.name: uses import type for TypeScript types
assertions.description: ReactElement/ReactNode must use import type to avoid Vite ESM/CJS issues

assertions.name: components use useTranslation() for i18n
assertions.description: Must use react-i18next, never hardcode strings

assertions.name: all three locale files exist (en-US, en-GB, it-IT)
assertions.description: Must generate en-US, en-GB, and it-IT translations with identical key structures

assertions.name: locales use nested structure
assertions.description: Each locale: locales/{locale}/translations.json (not flat)

assertions.name: tests directory exists
assertions.description: Test coverage enforcement requires test files

assertions.name: test files have vi.mock outside describe blocks
assertions.description: Vitest hoists mocks - must be outside blocks

assertions.name: configuration uses useConfig<AppConfig>() with direct property access
assertions.description: Must use useConfig<AppConfig>() with direct property access (appConfig.extension?.appName?.key || defaultValue), never .get() method. PUBLIC__app__extension__appName__key env vars

assertions.name: Apache 2.0 copyright headers with ISV name
assertions.description: All .ts/.tsx files must have Apache 2.0 license header with ISV/vendor name (not Salesforce) and current year

assertions.name: 'use client' directive after copyright header
assertions.description: Components must have 'use client' directive after copyright header, before imports

assertions.name: cartridges directory does NOT exist
assertions.description: UI-only shouldn't create backend

assertions.name: impex directory does NOT exist
assertions.description: UI-only doesn't need impex

assertions.name: no tsconfig.json at extension level
assertions.description: Extensions inherit TypeScript config from parent - no extension-level tsconfig

assertions.name: useTranslation namespace pattern
assertions.description: Namespace uses ext + PascalCase (e.g., 'extProductReviews'), JSON root key uses camelCase (e.g., 'productReviews')

assertions.name: uses 4-space indentation
assertions.description: All TypeScript, JSX, and JSON files use 4-space indentation (1 level = 4 spaces)

assertions.name: uses single quotes for strings
assertions.description: ESLint rule: single quotes for string literals (template literals allowed)

assertions.name: no hardcoded Tailwind color utilities
assertions.description: ESLint rule: use theme colors (bg-primary, text-foreground) not hardcoded (bg-blue-500)

assertions.name: no console statements
assertions.description: ESLint rule: no console.log, console.error in production code

evals.prompt: I'm building a tax service integration for TaxJar. This is backend only - hooks for tax calculation, service calls to their API, Business Manager configuration stuff. App name: taxjar-tax, v1.0.0, cartridge: int_taxjar_tax, service ID: taxjar.tax.api, domain is tax. Publisher: TaxJar Corp, https://developers.taxjar.com. We need to handle calculate, commit, and void for transactions.
evals.expected_output: Should create backend-only structure with cartridges, impex, hooks.json but NO storefront-next. Should configure tax domain hooks (app.tax.calculate, app.tax.commit, app.tax.cancel).

assertions.description: Backend needs service config for install/uninstall

assertions.name: hooks.json configured for tax domain
assertions.description: Should include app.tax.calculate, app.tax.commit, app.tax.cancel

assertions.name: storefront-next does NOT exist
assertions.description: Backend-only shouldn't create UI

assertions.name: cartridge name follows convention
assertions.description: Should use int_taxjar_tax format

assertions.name: commerce-app.json exists with correct metadata
assertions.description: All apps need manifest

evals.prompt: Build a UI-only wishlist feature - HeartList from WishCo, v1.0.0. Display a heart icon on product cards that users can click to save items. I need it to work in English (US and UK) and Italian since we're expanding to Europe. Make sure the code follows all the quality standards and best practices. Publisher: WishCo at https://wishco.io. Show the wishlist icon in the header before the cart icon.
evals.expected_output: Should create UI-only app with all three locales (en-US, en-GB, it-IT) and follow ESLint rules (single quotes, import type, no hardcoded colors, no console statements). Target config should use header.before.cart slot.

assertions.name: all three locale directories created
assertions.description: Must create en-US, en-GB, and it-IT locale directories

assertions.name: en-US translations.json exists
assertions.description: English (US) translations file created

assertions.name: en-GB translations.json exists
assertions.description: English (UK) translations file created

assertions.name: it-IT translations.json exists
assertions.description: Italian translations file created

assertions.name: all locale files have identical key structure
assertions.description: All three locale files must have the same JSON keys for consistency

assertions.name: Italian translations are different from English
assertions.description: it-IT file should have Italian text (Invia/Annulla), not English

assertions.name: uses single quotes consistently
assertions.description: All string literals use single quotes (ESLint rule)

assertions.name: all types use import type
assertions.description: ReactElement, ReactNode, and custom types use import type (ESLint rule)

assertions.name: no duplicate imports from same module
assertions.description: Imports from same module are combined into single statement (ESLint: no-duplicate-imports)

assertions.name: no array index used as React key
assertions.description: Components don't use array index as key prop in map() (ESLint: react/no-array-index-key)

assertions.name: no hardcoded Tailwind color classes
assertions.description: Uses theme colors (bg-primary, text-foreground) not hardcoded (bg-blue-500) (ESLint rule)

assertions.name: no console.log or console.error
assertions.description: No console statements in production code (ESLint rule)

assertions.name: max 120 characters per line
assertions.description: Lines don't exceed 120 characters (ESLint rule, ignoring URLs/long strings)

assertions.name: object shorthand used
assertions.description: Uses { name } not { name: name } where applicable (ESLint rule)

assertions.name: template literals for dynamic strings
assertions.description: Uses template literals over string concatenation (ESLint rule)

assertions.name: components are self-closing where appropriate
assertions.description: Empty components use <Component /> not <Component></Component> (ESLint rule)

assertions.name: trailing commas in multi-line structures
assertions.description: Objects, arrays, function params have trailing commas (Prettier rule)

assertions.name: parentheses around arrow function parameters
assertions.description: Single arrow params use parentheses: (item) => not item => (Prettier: arrowParens: 'always')

assertions.name: semicolons used consistently
assertions.description: All statements end with semicolons (Prettier: semi: true)

assertions.name: bracket spacing in objects
assertions.description: Spaces inside object brackets: { key: value } (Prettier: bracketSpacing: true)

assertions.name: JSX closing bracket same line
assertions.description: JSX closing > on same line as last prop (Prettier: bracketSameLine: true)

assertions.name: target-config.json uses header.before.cart
assertions.description: Uses correct targetId from Complete Target ID Reference
